summary: Feature request to expose SKCanvas.WritePixels() method to allow blitting pixel buffers directly to an SKSurface without creating an intermediate SKImage each frame.

reproEvidence.environmentDetails: No specific platform mentioned — general SkiaSharp API request
codeSnippets:
// Current pattern: decompress JIT into pixel buffer, create SKImage, DrawImage, dispose
// Requested: use SKCanvas.WritePixels() to blit pixel buffer directly to surface

analysis.summary: Reporter wants to avoid creating an SKImage middleman on every animation frame when blitting a raw pixel buffer to a reused SKSurface. They request exposure of Skia's SkCanvas::writePixels() C++ method, which writes pixel data directly to the canvas surface. This method exists in the upstream Skia C++ API but is not currently exposed in SkiaSharp's SKCanvas C# class or the underlying C API shim.
analysis.rationale: The issue is clearly a feature request: no bug or broken behavior is described. The reporter has a working workaround (DrawImage) but wants a more efficient path. Skia's SkCanvas::writePixels() is a real upstream API, confirmed absent from the SkiaSharp C# binding and C API shim. The existing SKSurface.ReadPixels and similar pixel-access patterns confirm the plumbing style that would be needed.

codeInvestigation.finding: SKCanvas.cs has a TODO comment mentioning PeekPixels and ReadPixels consideration but no WritePixels method is present. The file has DrawImage methods but no direct pixel-write path.
SKSurface.ReadPixels exists, showing the existing pattern for pixel access. A symmetric WritePixels on SKCanvas would follow the same pattern using an SKPixmap or IntPtr + SKImageInfo.

keySignals.text: Please expose SKIA's SKCanvas.WritePixels() method as is.
keySignals.interpretation: Direct request for a named Skia API — SkCanvas::writePixels() — which is not yet wrapped in SkiaSharp.
Performance motivation: reporter wants to avoid per-frame allocation of SKImage objects in a tight animation loop.

keySignals.text: we are creating SKImage from it, each frame
keySignals.interpretation: Current workaround is functional but allocates per frame; WritePixels would be allocation-free.

workarounds: Use SKSurface.Canvas.DrawBitmap(bitmap, 0, 0) with a pre-allocated SKBitmap whose pixels are updated in-place using SKBitmap.SetPixels(ptr) — avoids per-frame SKImage allocation.
Use SKPixmap wrapping the existing pixel buffer and call SKSurface.Canvas.DrawImage(SKImage.FromPixels(pixmap), ...) — still creates SKImage but it is a zero-copy wrappers if the buffer is pinned.
Use SKBitmap backed by an unmanaged pixel buffer (SKBitmap.InstallPixels) and draw that bitmap directly — avoids repeated creation of new SKBitmap objects.

resolution.hypothesis: Adding SKCanvas.WritePixels() requires: (1) a C API function sk_canvas_write_pixels in externals/skia/include/c/sk_canvas.h and externals/skia/src/c/sk_canvas.cpp wrapping SkCanvas::writePixels(); (2) a P/Invoke declaration in SkiaApi.generated.cs; (3) public overloads on SKCanvas in binding/SkiaSharp/SKCanvas.cs accepting (SKImageInfo, IntPtr, int rowBytes) and/or (SKPixmap).

proposals.title: Workaround: use SKBitmap.InstallPixels + DrawBitmap
proposals.description: Pin the pixel buffer using GCHandle or unmanaged memory, call SKBitmap.InstallPixels to wrap it without copying, then call canvas.DrawBitmap each frame. The SKBitmap is created once and reused.

proposals.title: Implement SKCanvas.WritePixels()
proposals.description: Add C API wrapper sk_canvas_write_pixels, regenerate P/Invoke, and add SKCanvas.WritePixels overloads. Mirrors the pattern of SKSurface.ReadPixels.

resolution.recommendedReason: Immediate workaround available now with no SkiaSharp changes required. The real fix (exposing WritePixels) is a valid feature request worth implementing but requires C API work.

nextQuestions: Does the reporter's use case require offset writes (non-zero srcX/srcY)?
Is there interest in also wrapping SkSurface::writePixels() in addition to SkCanvas::writePixels()?

actionability.reason: Valid feature request — SkCanvas::writePixels() is a real upstream API not yet exposed. A workaround exists (SKBitmap.InstallPixels + DrawBitmap) but the feature itself is worthwhile for performance-sensitive animation scenarios.

actions.description: Confirm feature-request label and add area/SkiaSharp and tenet/performance
Acknowledge request and share workaround using SKBitmap.InstallPixels
